tools.name: TIME_SERIES_INTRADAY
tools.description: Returns current and 20+ years of historical intraday OHLCV time series of the equity specified.

symbol.description: The name of the equity. For example: symbol=IBM

interval.description: Time interval between consecutive data points. Supported: 1min, 5min, 15min, 30min, 60min

adjusted.description: By default True. Set False to query raw (as-traded) intraday values

extended_hours.description: By default True. Set False for regular trading hours only

month.description: Query specific month in YYYY-MM format. Example: 2009-01

outputsize.description: "compact" (100 data points) or "full" (30 days or full month)

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: TIME_SERIES_DAILY
tools.description: Returns raw daily time series (OHLCV) of the global equity specified, covering 20+ years of historical data.

symbol.description: The name of the equity. For example: symbol=IBM

outputsize.description: "compact" (100 data points) or "full" (20+ years of historical data)

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: TIME_SERIES_DAILY_ADJUSTED
tools.description: Returns raw daily OHLCV values, adjusted close values, and historical split/dividend events.

symbol.description: The name of the equity. For example: symbol=IBM

outputsize.description: "compact" (100 data points) or "full" (20+ years of historical data)

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: TIME_SERIES_WEEKLY
tools.description: Returns weekly time series (last trading day of each week, OHLCV) covering 20+ years of historical data.

symbol.description: The name of the equity. For example: symbol=IBM

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: TIME_SERIES_WEEKLY_ADJUSTED
tools.description: Returns weekly adjusted time series (OHLCV, adjusted close, volume, dividend) covering 20+ years.

symbol.description: The name of the equity. For example: symbol=IBM

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: TIME_SERIES_MONTHLY
tools.description: Returns monthly time series (last trading day of each month, OHLCV) covering 20+ years.

symbol.description: The name of the equity. For example: symbol=IBM

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: TIME_SERIES_MONTHLY_ADJUSTED
tools.description: Returns monthly adjusted time series (OHLCV, adjusted close, volume, dividend) covering 20+ years.

symbol.description: The name of the equity. For example: symbol=IBM

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: GLOBAL_QUOTE
tools.description: Returns the latest price and volume information for a ticker.

symbol.description: The symbol of the global ticker. For example: symbol=IBM

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: REALTIME_BULK_QUOTES
tools.description: Returns realtime quotes for US-traded symbols in bulk, accepting up to 100 symbols per request.

symbol.description: Up to 100 symbols separated by comma. Example: MSFT,AAPL,IBM

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: SYMBOL_SEARCH
tools.description: Returns best-matching symbols and market information based on keywords.

keywords.description: A text string of your choice. Example: microsoft

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: MARKET_STATUS
tools.description: Returns the current market status (open vs. closed) of major trading venues worldwide.

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: REALTIME_OPTIONS
tools.description: Returns realtime US options data with full market coverage. Option chains are sorted by expiration dates in chronological order. Within the same expiration date, contracts are sorted by strike prices from low to high.

symbol.description: The name of the equity of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM

require_greeks.description: Enable greeks & implied volatility (IV) fields. By default, require_greeks=false.

contract.description: The US options contract ID you would like to specify. By default, the contract parameter

expiration.description: The expiration date in YYYY-MM-DD format. The expiration date must be on or after today's date.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: REALTIME_OPTIONS_FMV
tools.description: Returns realtime FMV (fair market value) mark prices for US options. Option chains are sorted by expiration dates in chronological order. Within the same expiration date, contracts are sorted by strike prices from low to high.

symbol.description: The name of the equity of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM

require_greeks.description: Enable greeks & implied volatility (IV) fields alongside the FMV mark.

contract.description: The US options contract ID you would like to specify. By default, the contract parameter

expiration.description: The expiration date in YYYY-MM-DD format. The expiration date must be on or after today's date.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: REALTIME_PUT_CALL_RATIO
tools.description: Returns the realtime put-call ratio for US-traded equities and ETFs. The put-call ratio is calculated for the entire option chain as well as for each specific expiration date. A put-call ratio equal to or less than 0.6 typically signals a bullish market sentiment, while a ratio equal to or greater than 1.0 signals a bearish sentiment.

symbol.description: The name of the equity of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM

tools.name: REALTIME_VOLUME_OPEN_INTEREST_RATIO
tools.description: Returns the realtime volume-to-open-interest ratio within an option chain. A high ratio (volume much larger than open interest) often suggests heavy trading activity relative to existing positions, which can indicate short-term speculation, increased liquidity, or possible trend changes. A low ratio (volume small compared to open interest) implies that most positions are being held rather than actively traded, signaling more stable or less volatile conditions.

symbol.description: The name of the equity of your choice. For example: symbol=NVDA

tools.name: HISTORICAL_OPTIONS
tools.description: Returns the full historical options chain for a specific symbol on a specific date. Covers 15+ years of history. Implied volatility (IV) and common Greeks (e.g., delta, gamma, theta, vega, rho) are also returned. Option chains are sorted by expiration dates in chronological order. Within the same expiration date, contracts are sorted by strike prices from low to high.

symbol.description: The name of the equity of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM

date.description: By default, the date parameter is not set and the API will return data for the previous trading session.

contract.description: The US options contract ID you would like to specify. By default, the contract parameter

expiration.description: The contract expiration date in YYYY-MM-DD format. By default, expiration is not set and

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: HISTORICAL_PUT_CALL_RATIO
tools.description: Returns the historical put-call ratio for US-traded equities and ETFs. The put-call ratio is calculated for the entire option chain as well as for each specific expiration date. Covers data from 2008-01-01 to the previous trading session.

symbol.description: The name of the equity of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM

date.description: The date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Any date later than 2008-01-01 is accepted.

tools.name: HISTORICAL_VOLUME_OPEN_INTEREST_RATIO
tools.description: Returns the historical volume-to-open-interest ratio within an option chain. A high ratio (volume much larger than open interest) often suggests heavy trading activity relative to existing positions, which can indicate short-term speculation, increased liquidity, or possible trend changes. A low ratio (volume small compared to open interest) implies that most positions are being held rather than actively traded, signaling more stable or less volatile conditions.

symbol.description: The name of the equity of your choice. For example: symbol=NVDA

date.description: The date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Any date later than 2008-01-01 is accepted.

tools.name: NEWS_SENTIMENT
tools.description: Returns live and historical market news & sentiment data from premier news outlets worldwide. Covers stocks, cryptocurrencies, forex, and topics like fiscal policy, mergers & acquisitions, IPOs.

tickers.description: Stock/crypto/forex symbols to filter articles. Example: "IBM" or "COIN,CRYPTO:BTC,FOREX:USD".

topics.description: News topics to filter by. Example: "technology" or "technology,ipo".

time_from.description: Start time range in YYYYMMDDTHHMM format. Example: "20220410T0130".

time_to.description: End time range in YYYYMMDDTHHMM format. Defaults to current time if time_from specified.

limit.description: Number of results to return. Default 50, max 1000.

tools.name: EARNINGS_CALL_TRANSCRIPT
tools.description: Returns earnings call transcript for a company in a specific quarter. Covers 15+ years of history enriched with LLM-based sentiment signals.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol. Example: "IBM".

quarter.description: Fiscal quarter in YYYYQM format. Example: "2024Q1". Supports quarters since 2010Q1.

tools.name: TOP_GAINERS_LOSERS
tools.description: Returns top 20 gainers, losers, and most active traded tickers in the US market.

entitlement.description: "delayed" for 15-minute delayed data, "realtime" for realtime data

tools.name: INSIDER_TRANSACTIONS
tools.description: Returns latest and historical insider transactions by key stakeholders. Covers transactions by founders, executives, board members, etc.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol. Example: "IBM".

from_date.description: Optional start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Only return transactions on or after this date. Example: "2026-03-01".

tools.name: INSTITUTIONAL_HOLDINGS
tools.description: Returns institutional ownership and holdings information for an equity.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol. Example: "IBM".

tools.name: ANALYTICS_FIXED_WINDOW
tools.description: Returns advanced analytics metrics for time series over a fixed temporal window. Calculates metrics like total return, variance, auto-correlation, etc.

symbols.description: Comma-separated list of symbols. Free keys: up to 5, Premium keys: up to 50.

range_param.description: Date range for the series. Defaults to full equity history.

interval.description: Time interval - 1min, 5min, 15min, 30min, 60min, DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY.

calculations.description: Comma-separated list of analytics metrics to calculate.

ohlc.description: OHLC field for calculation - open, high, low, close. Default "close".

tools.name: ANALYTICS_SLIDING_WINDOW
tools.description: Returns advanced analytics metrics for time series over sliding time windows. Calculates moving metrics like variance over time periods. Example: moving variance over 5 years with 100-point window.

symbols.description: Comma-separated list of symbols. Free keys: up to 5, Premium keys: up to 50.

range_param.description: Date range for the series. Defaults to full equity history.

interval.description: Time interval - 1min, 5min, 15min, 30min, 60min, DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY.

window_size.description: Size of moving window. Minimum 10, larger recommended for statistical significance.

calculations.description: Comma-separated analytics metrics. Free keys: 1 metric, Premium keys: multiple.

ohlc.description: OHLC field for calculation - open, high, low, close. Default "close".

tools.name: GOLD_SILVER_SPOT
tools.description: Returns the live spot prices of gold and silver metals.

symbol.description: For gold, strings GOLD and XAU are accepted. For silver, strings SILVER and XAG are accepted.

tools.name: GOLD_SILVER_HISTORY
tools.description: Returns the historical gold and silver prices in daily, weekly, and monthly horizons.

symbol.description: For gold, strings GOLD and XAU are accepted. For silver, strings SILVER and XAG are accepted.

interval.description: By default, monthly. Strings daily, weekly, and monthly are accepted.

datatype.description: By default, csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: CURRENCY_EXCHANGE_RATE
tools.description: Returns the realtime exchange rate for any pair of digital currency (e.g., Bitcoin) or physical currency (e.g., USD).

from_currency.description: The currency you would like to get the exchange rate for. It can either be a physical currency or digital/crypto currency. For example: from_currency=USD or from_currency=BTC.

to_currency.description: The destination currency for the exchange rate. It can either be a physical currency or digital/crypto currency. For example: to_currency=USD or to_currency=BTC.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications: json returns the exchange rate in JSON format; csv returns the data as a CSV (comma separated value) file.

tools.name: CRYPTO_INTRADAY
tools.description: Returns intraday time series (timestamp, open, high, low, close, volume) of the cryptocurrency specified, updated realtime.

symbol.description: The digital/crypto currency of your choice. It can be any of the currencies in the digital currency list. For example: symbol=ETH.

market.description: The exchange market of your choice. It can be any of the market in the market list. For example: market=USD.

interval.description: Time interval between two consecutive data points in the time series. The following values are supported: 1min, 5min, 15min, 30min, 60min

outputsize.description: By default, outputsize=compact. Strings compact and full are accepted with the following specifications: compact returns only the latest 100 data points in the intraday time series; full returns the full-length intraday time series. The "compact" option is recommended if you would like to reduce the data size of each API call.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications: json returns the intraday time series in JSON format; csv returns the time series as a CSV (comma separated value) file.

tools.name: DIGITAL_CURRENCY_DAILY
tools.description: Returns the daily historical time series for a digital currency (e.g., BTC) traded on a specific market (e.g., EUR/Euro), refreshed daily at midnight (UTC). Prices and volumes are quoted in both the market-specific currency and USD.

symbol.description: The digital/crypto currency of your choice. It can be any of the currencies in the digital currency list. For example: symbol=BTC.

market.description: The exchange market of your choice. It can be any of the market in the market list. For example: market=EUR.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications: json returns the daily time series in JSON format; csv returns the data as a CSV (comma separated value) file.

tools.name: DIGITAL_CURRENCY_WEEKLY
tools.description: Returns the weekly historical time series for a digital currency (e.g., BTC) traded on a specific market (e.g., EUR/Euro), refreshed daily at midnight (UTC). Prices and volumes are quoted in both the market-specific currency and USD.

symbol.description: The digital/crypto currency of your choice. It can be any of the currencies in the digital currency list. For example: symbol=BTC.

market.description: The exchange market of your choice. It can be any of the market in the market list. For example: market=EUR.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications: json returns the weekly time series in JSON format; csv returns the data as a CSV (comma separated value) file.

tools.name: DIGITAL_CURRENCY_MONTHLY
tools.description: Returns the monthly historical time series for a digital currency (e.g., BTC) traded on a specific market (e.g., EUR/Euro), refreshed daily at midnight (UTC). Prices and volumes are quoted in both the market-specific currency and USD.

symbol.description: The digital/crypto currency of your choice. It can be any of the currencies in the digital currency list. For example: symbol=BTC.

market.description: The exchange market of your choice. It can be any of the market in the market list. For example: market=EUR.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications: json returns the monthly time series in JSON format; csv returns the data as a CSV (comma separated value) file.

tools.name: REAL_GDP
tools.description: Returns the annual and quarterly Real GDP of the United States.

interval.description: By default, interval=annual. Strings quarterly and annual are accepted.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: REAL_GDP_PER_CAPITA
tools.description: Returns the quarterly Real GDP per Capita data of the United States.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: TREASURY_YIELD
tools.description: Returns the daily, weekly, and monthly US treasury yield of a given maturity timeline (e.g., 5 year, 30 year, etc).

interval.description: By default, interval=monthly. Strings daily, weekly, and monthly are accepted.

maturity.description: By default, maturity=10year. Strings 3month, 2year, 5year, 7year, 10year, and 30year are accepted.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: FEDERAL_FUNDS_RATE
tools.description: Returns the daily, weekly, and monthly federal funds rate (interest rate) of the United States.

interval.description: By default, interval=monthly. Strings daily, weekly, and monthly are accepted.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: CPI
tools.description: Returns the monthly and semiannual consumer price index (CPI) of the United States. CPI is widely regarded as the barometer of inflation levels in the broader economy.

interval.description: By default, interval=monthly. Strings monthly and semiannual are accepted.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: INFLATION
tools.description: Returns the annual inflation rates (consumer prices) of the United States.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: RETAIL_SALES
tools.description: Returns the monthly Advance Retail Sales: Retail Trade data of the United States.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: DURABLES
tools.description: Returns the monthly manufacturers' new orders of durable goods in the United States.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: UNEMPLOYMENT
tools.description: Returns the monthly unemployment data of the United States. The unemployment rate represents the number of unemployed as a percentage of the labor force. Labor force data are restricted to people 16 years of age and older, who currently reside in 1 of the 50 states or the District of Columbia, who do not reside in institutions (e.g., penal and mental facilities, homes for the aged), and who are not on active duty in the Armed Forces.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: NONFARM_PAYROLL
tools.description: Returns the monthly US All Employees: Total Nonfarm (commonly known as Total Nonfarm Payroll), a measure of the number of U.S. workers in the economy that excludes proprietors, private household employees, unpaid volunteers, farm employees, and the unincorporated self-employed.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: FX_INTRADAY
tools.description: Returns intraday time series (timestamp, open, high, low, close) of the FX currency pair specified, updated realtime.

from_symbol.description: A three-letter symbol from the forex currency list. For example: from_symbol=EUR

to_symbol.description: A three-letter symbol from the forex currency list. For example: to_symbol=USD

interval.description: Time interval between two consecutive data points in the time series. The following values are supported: 1min, 5min, 15min, 30min, 60min

outputsize.description: By default, outputsize=compact. Strings compact and full are accepted with the following specifications:

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: FX_DAILY
tools.description: Returns the daily time series (timestamp, open, high, low, close) of the FX currency pair specified, updated realtime.

from_symbol.description: A three-letter symbol from the forex currency list. For example: from_symbol=EUR

to_symbol.description: A three-letter symbol from the forex currency list. For example: to_symbol=USD

outputsize.description: By default, outputsize=compact. Strings compact and full are accepted with the following specifications:

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: FX_WEEKLY
tools.description: Returns the weekly time series (timestamp, open, high, low, close) of the FX currency pair specified, updated realtime. The latest data point is the price information for the week (or partial week) containing the current trading day, updated realtime.

from_symbol.description: A three-letter symbol from the forex currency list. For example: from_symbol=EUR

to_symbol.description: A three-letter symbol from the forex currency list. For example: to_symbol=USD

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: FX_MONTHLY
tools.description: Returns the monthly time series (timestamp, open, high, low, close) of the FX currency pair specified, updated realtime. The latest data point is the prices information for the month (or partial month) containing the current trading day, updated realtime.

from_symbol.description: A three-letter symbol from the forex currency list. For example: from_symbol=EUR

to_symbol.description: A three-letter symbol from the forex currency list. For example: to_symbol=USD

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted with the following specifications:

tools.name: COMPANY_OVERVIEW
tools.description: Returns company information, financial ratios, and key metrics for the specified equity. Data is generally refreshed on the same day a company reports its latest earnings and financials.

symbol.description: The symbol of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM.

tools.name: ETF_PROFILE
tools.description: Returns key ETF metrics and holdings with allocation by asset types and sectors. Includes net assets, expense ratio, turnover, and corresponding ETF holdings/constituents.

symbol.description: The symbol of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=QQQ.

tools.name: DIVIDENDS
tools.description: Returns historical and future (declared) dividend distributions.

symbol.description: The symbol of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted.

tools.name: SPLITS
tools.description: Returns historical split events.

symbol.description: The symbol of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM.

datatype.description: By default, datatype=csv. Strings json and csv are accepted.

tools.name: INCOME_STATEMENT
tools.description: Returns annual and quarterly income statements with normalized fields. Fields are mapped to GAAP and IFRS taxonomies of the SEC. Data is generally refreshed on the same day a company reports its latest earnings and financials.

symbol.description: The symbol of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM.

tools.name: BALANCE_SHEET
tools.description: Returns annual and quarterly balance sheets with normalized fields. Fields are mapped to GAAP and IFRS taxonomies of the SEC. Data is generally refreshed on the same day a company reports its latest earnings and financials.

symbol.description: The symbol of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM.

tools.name: CASH_FLOW
tools.description: Returns annual and quarterly cash flow with normalized fields. Fields are mapped to GAAP and IFRS taxonomies of the SEC. Data is generally refreshed on the same day a company reports its latest earnings and financials.

symbol.description: The symbol of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM.

tools.name: EARNINGS
tools.description: Returns annual and quarterly earnings (EPS) for the company. Quarterly data also includes analyst estimates and surprise metrics.

symbol.description: The symbol of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM.

tools.name: EARNINGS_ESTIMATES
tools.description: Returns annual and quarterly EPS and revenue estimates with analyst data. Includes analyst count and revision history.

symbol.description: The symbol of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM.

tools.name: LISTING_STATUS
tools.description: Returns a list of active or delisted US stocks and ETFs. Can return data as of the latest trading day or at a specific time in history. Facilitates equity research on asset lifecycle and survivorship.

date.description: If no date is set, returns symbols as of the latest trading day.

state.description: By default, state=active returns actively traded stocks and ETFs.

tools.name: EARNINGS_CALENDAR
tools.description: Returns a list of company earnings expected in the next 3, 6, or 12 months.

symbol.description: By default, no symbol is set and returns full list of scheduled earnings.

horizon.description: By default, horizon=3month returns earnings in the next 3 months.

tools.name: IPO_CALENDAR
tools.description: Returns a list of IPOs expected in the next 3 months.

tools.name: SMA
tools.description: Returns the simple moving average (SMA) values.

symbol.description: The name of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM

interval.description: Time interval between two consecutive data points in the time series.

time_period.description: Number of data points used to calculate each moving average value.

series_type.description: The desired price type in the time series. Four types are supported: close, open, high, low

tools.name: BBANDS
tools.description: Returns the Bollinger bands (BBANDS) values.

symbol.description: The name of the ticker of your choice. For example: symbol=IBM

interval.description: Time interval between two consecutive data points in the time series.

time_period.description: Number of data points used to calculate each BBANDS value. Positive integers are accepted.

series_type.description: The desired price type in the time series. Four types are supported: close, open, high, low

nbdevup.description: The standard deviation multiplier of the upper band. Positive integers are accepted. By default, nbdevup=2.

nbdevdn.description: The standard deviation multiplier of the lower band. Positive integers are accepted. By default, nbdevdn=2.

matype.description: Moving average type of the time series. By default, matype=0. Integers 0-8 are accepted with the following mappings:

month.description: Note: this parameter is ONLY applicable to intraday intervals (1min, 5min, 15min, 30min, and 60min)

tools.name: PING
tools.description: Check if the service is healthy.

tools.name: ADD_TWO_NUMBERS
tools.description: Add two numbers together.

tools.name: INDEX_DATA
tools.description: Returns daily, weekly, or monthly OHLC time series data for 200+ major market indices (e.g., DJI, SPX, COMP, NDX, VIX, RUT). For the full list of supported indices, use INDEX_CATALOG.

symbol.description: The index symbol (e.g., DJI for Dow Jones, SPX for S&P 500, COMP for NASDAQ Composite, NDX for NASDAQ 100, VIX for Cboe Volatility Index, RUT for Russell 2000).

interval.description: Time interval between two data points. Supported values: daily, weekly, monthly.

datatype.description: By default, json. Strings json and csv are accepted.

tools.name: INDEX_CATALOG
tools.description: Returns the full list of supported index symbols with their long-form names.

datatype.description: By default, json. Strings json and csv are accepted.

tools.name: TOOL_LIST
tools.description: Lists the available Alpha Vantage API tools, returning each tool's name and short description without parameter schemas.

tools.name: TOOL_GET
tools.description: Returns the full schema for one or more named tools, including each tool's name, description, and parameter definitions (JSON schema). Accepts a single tool name or a list of tool names.

tool_name.description: The name of the tool to get schema for (e.g., "TIME_SERIES_DAILY"),

tools.name: TOOL_CALL
tools.description: Executes a named Alpha Vantage API tool with the provided arguments and returns its result.

tool_name.description: The name of the tool to call (e.g., "TIME_SERIES_DAILY")

arguments.description: Dictionary of arguments matching the tool's parameter schema
